key_points: Erbium lasers (Er:YAG, Er,Cr:YSGG) demonstrate effective and selective caries ablation, smear layer removal, and improved adhesion in restorative dentistry.

Laser-activated irrigation protocols such as photon-induced photoacoustic streaming (PIPS) and shock wave-enhanced emission photoacoustic streaming (SWEEPS) enhance irrigant penetration and biofilm disruption in endodontics.
In prosthodontics and orthodontics, erbium lasers allow minimally invasive debonding of crowns, veneers, and brackets while preserving underlying structures.
In periodontology and peri-implantitis, lasers improve debridement, support regeneration, and enhance wound healing.
Photobiomodulation (PBM/LLLT) contributes to pain reduction, inflammation control, and tissue repair.
Outcomes vary with wavelength, energy settings, and clinical protocols, underscoring the need for standardization.
The review is aligned with the FDI Policy Statement on dental lasers to support globally consistent clinical practice.
Further high-quality randomized controlled trials are necessary to define standardized protocols across dental laser applications.
notes: Narrative review integrating original studies, systematic reviews, and consensus statements from PubMed, Scopus, and Web of Science. Covers multiple dental specialties. No original clinical data reported.
source_citation: Grzech-Leśniak, K. (2026). Lasers in Dentistry - Overview Based on FDI Policy Statement. International Dental Journal. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.identj.2025.109369
clinical_indication: Multiple dental applications: caries removal, endodontic disinfection, periodontal therapy, peri-implant therapy, prosthetic and orthodontic debonding, photobiomodulation for pain and inflammation
target_tissue: dentin, enamel, pulp, gingiva, bone, periodontal tissue, peri-implant tissue
uncertainties: No specific laser parameters (power, fluence, pulse duration, frequency) are reported in the abstract; full text may contain some but this is a narrative review without original data.
Conflicts of interest and funding sources not declared in the available metadata.
The review is narrative in nature, so evidence synthesis methodology (e.g., risk of bias assessment) is not described, limiting evidence level assessment.
